allow: Bash(cmd /c "gemini -p ""hello, please respond with just the word ''working''""")

Bash("C:\\Users\\toast\\.bun\\bin\\gemini.exe" -p "I need your help integrating the content from the file ''2026-01-17 01-55-12.txt'' into our modular framework. This txt file contains a comprehensive AI Writing Directives guide with sections on: story structure, character construction, POV, prose mechanics, pacing, beginnings/endings, genre-specific guidance, and common AI pitfalls. How should we map this content to our existing modules \(_ANTI_AI_CORE.md, _HUMAN_PATTERNS.md, _WORD_VARIATION.md, _FICTION_POV.md, _ROMANCE_HEAT.md, _BIOGRAPHY.md\) and what new modules might we need to create? Please analyze and provide a recommended integration plan.")

Bash("C:\\Users\\toast\\.bun\\bin\\gemini.exe" -p "I''ve completed the integration of the AI Writing Directives from the txt file into our modular framework. Please review all the changes and confirm everything looks good. The updates include: 1\) Updated existing modules \(_ANTI_AI_CORE, _HUMAN_PATTERNS, _WORD_VARIATION, _FICTION_POV, _ROMANCE_HEAT\) 2\) Created 4 new modules \(_STORY_ARCHITECT, _CHARACTER_CRAFT, _GENRE_PLAYBOOK, _WRITING_WORKFLOW\) 3\) Added CLI communication instructions to CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, and collaboration_workflow.md. Please review and provide any feedback.")

Bash("C:\\Users\\toast\\.bun\\bin\\gemini.exe" -p "I need your help reviewing the _New_Book_Starter_Kit writing framework for weaknesses and gaps. I''ve thoroughly analyzed all modules. Here''s what we have:

FOUNDATION LAYER \(Always Active\):
1. _ANTI_AI_CORE.md - Banned words/phrases, AI pitfalls, filter words
2. _HUMAN_PATTERNS.md - Rhythm, burstiness, sentence variation
3. _WRITING_WORKFLOW.md - Process, Claude-Gemini collaboration, checklists

FICTION TRACK:
4. _STORY_ENGINE.md - Want/Need, stakes, structure templates
5. _CHARACTER_CRAFT.md - Arcs, dimensionality, voice
6. _PACING_AND_STRUCTURE.md - Scene goals, openings, endings
7. _FICTION_POV.md - POV discipline, dialogue rules
8. _GENRE_PLAYBOOK.md - Genre-specific beats
9. _ROMANCE_HEAT.md - Heat levels, romantasy rules

NONFICTION TRACK:
10. _NONFICTION_CORE.md - Verifiability, structure, voice
11. _BIOGRAPHY.md - Narrative biography approach

ENHANCEMENT:
12. _WORD_VARIATION.md - Crutch word limits
13. KDP_BOOK_FORMATTING_SKILL.md - KDP publishing formatting

Please identify:
1. GAPS - What''s missing from this framework?
2. CONTRADICTIONS - Any conflicting rules between modules?
3. WEAK MODULES - Which modules need strengthening?
4. OVERLAPS - Redundant content that should be consolidated?
5. MISSING GENRES - Any genres not adequately covered?
6. WORKFLOW ISSUES - Problems with the Claude-Gemini collaboration model?
7. PRACTICAL ISSUES - Rules that are too vague or unenforceable?

Be ruthless. We want this framework to be bulletproof.")

Bash("C:\\Users\\toast\\.bun\\bin\\gemini.exe" -p "I''ve completed major revisions to the _New_Book_Starter_Kit writing framework based on your review. Here''s what changed:

PHASE 1 - CONSOLIDATION:
- Created _STYLE_AUTHORITY.md \(merged _ANTI_AI_CORE.md + _WORD_VARIATION.md\)
- Resolved banned words contradiction with ''pervasive pattern'' framing
- Moved KDP_BOOK_FORMATTING_SKILL.md to reference/
- Removed romance redundancy from Genre Playbook

PHASE 2 - CORE IMPROVEMENTS:
- Renamed _FICTION_POV.md to _NARRATIVE_VOICE.md with gender/tense neutral examples
- Created new _DIALOGUE_CRAFT.md module \(subtext, blocking, voice differentiation\)
- Expanded _GENRE_PLAYBOOK.md from ~40 lines to ~350 lines
- Added Horror, Comedy/Humor, Middle Grade, YA sections

PHASE 3 - NEW CONTENT:
- Created _WORLDBUILDING.md \(setting, magic systems, culture, info-dump alternatives\)
- Added comprehensive Act 2 guidance to _PACING_AND_STRUCTURE.md
- Created _REVISION_TOOLKIT.md \(Problem → Diagnosis → Fix patterns\)

PHASE 4 - FLEXIBILITY:
- Added three tone modes to _BIOGRAPHY.md \(Warm, Critical, Dark\)
- Added Session Initialization protocol to _WRITING_WORKFLOW.md
- Updated CLAUDE.md with new module structure

ARCHIVED TO backup/:
- _ANTI_AI_CORE.md
- _WORD_VARIATION.md

Please review and identify:
1. Any remaining gaps or weak points?
2. Any contradictions introduced by the changes?
3. Cross-references that need updating?
4. Overall assessment of improvements?

Be thorough - this is the final QA pass.")

Bash("C:\\Users\\toast\\.bun\\bin\\gemini.exe" -p "Please do a COMPREHENSIVE review of the entire _New_Book_Starter_Kit writing framework. Read ALL module files in the modules/ directory and check for:

1. INTERNAL CONSISTENCY - Do modules contradict each other? Are rules applied consistently?

2. COMPLETENESS - Are there any gaps in coverage? Missing scenarios? Incomplete guidance?

3. CLARITY - Are instructions clear and actionable? Any ambiguous rules?

4. CROSS-REFERENCES - Do all module references point to files that exist? Any broken links?

5. PRACTICAL USABILITY - Would an AI actually be able to follow these rules in practice?

6. REDUNDANCY - Any remaining duplicate content between modules?

7. HIERARCHY - Is the [STRICT] vs [FLEXIBLE] tagging consistent? Are priorities clear?

8. EDGE CASES - What happens when genres blend? When fiction needs nonfiction research? When rules conflict?

Read every file in modules/ and provide a detailed assessment. Be ruthlessly thorough - we want this framework bulletproof.")

Bash("C:\\Users\\toast\\.bun\\bin\\gemini.exe" -p "Final verification of the _New_Book_Starter_Kit framework after additional fixes:

FIXES IMPLEMENTED:
1. Added ''Narrative Nonfiction'' hybrid track to _WRITING_WORKFLOW.md \(loads _NONFICTION_CORE + fiction craft modules for Memoir/True Crime/Immersive Journalism\)

2. Added comprehensive ''Memoir'' section to _GENRE_PLAYBOOK.md \(~60 lines\) covering:
   - Memoir vs Autobiography distinction
   - Then-self vs Now-self narrator
   - Scene construction with nonfiction ethics
   - Ethical boundaries [STRICT]

3. Added ''Contemporary & Micro-Settings'' section to _WORLDBUILDING.md covering:
   - Corporate offices, high schools, small towns, etc.
   - Insider language and unwritten rules
   - Era-specific contemporary \(2003 vs 2008\)
   - Research integration for unfamiliar settings

4. Added ''Showing Without Inventing'' section to _NONFICTION_CORE.md covering:
   - Sources for sensory details \(weather, setting, dialogue\)
   - Techniques for vivid nonfiction without fabrication
   - Confidence level signaling
   - What you cannot do [STRICT]

Please verify:
1. Do these additions resolve the gaps you identified?
2. Any new contradictions introduced?
3. Is the framework now complete?

This is the FINAL check before declaring the framework production-ready.")
